behaviors.description: Only files changed in a pull request are passed to the linter; unchanged files are excluded from analysis.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on correctly invoking git (or GitHub API) to retrieve the set of changed files. A Unit test with mocked file lists would not reveal failures in subprocess invocation (wrong branch, wrong arguments, malformed output parsing). The real failure boundary is at the subprocess/network level where the changed-files list is actually obtained.
behaviors.test_description: Set up a temporary git repository with multiple commits and test files. Mark some files as changed in HEAD, others unchanged. Run the lint step and capture which files are passed to the linter. Verify only changed files are analyzed; unchanged files are never processed.

behaviors.description: Linter findings are grouped by severity level (error, warning, info) in the posted PR review comment.
behaviors.justification: The core behavior (grouping findings by severity in the message) can be verified by mocking the GitHub API client and inspecting the request body/message text. A Unit test would miss the formatting step of composing the full comment body. Testing at Integration level reveals failures in message composition without requiring real HTTP to GitHub.
behaviors.test_description: Create sample linter findings with mixed severity levels (errors, warnings, info). Mock the GitHub API comment-posting method to capture the message body. Run the lint step. Verify the posted message groups findings by severity with clear headers and no findings appearing in wrong groups.

behaviors.description: Linting analysis of 200 changed files completes within the 10-minute GitHub Actions timeout.
behaviors.justification: Performance behavior can only be measured with real linter subprocesses running on real files. A mocked linter (returning instantly) would hide actual performance bottlenecks, timeouts, or I/O delays. Wall-clock execution time of actual subprocess calls is the real failure boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Generate or prepare 200 test source files (varied types/sizes). Run the complete lint step analyzing all 200 files. Measure elapsed time from start to finish. Verify total execution is under 10 minutes. Document and analyze slow subprocess stages for bottleneck identification.

behaviors.description: When the linter process crashes on a malformed input file, error handling recovers and continues analyzing remaining files.
behaviors.justification: The core behavior (error handling logic catches crashes and continues) can be tested by simulating a linter crash: mock the subprocess to raise an exception or return a non-zero exit code on a specific file. This tests whether the error-handling code works correctly without requiring a real linter crash. The real failure is in error handling logic, not linter-specific behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the linter subprocess to crash (raise exception or return non-zero exit) on a specific file (e.g. 'malformed.js'). Create a test batch with one crash-triggering file and several normal files. Run the lint step. Verify: (1) crash is caught/logged, (2) remaining files complete analysis successfully, (3) results are returned for non-crashed files.

behaviors.description: Duplicate findings from multiple rules on the same line are de-duplicated so each issue appears only once in the output.
behaviors.justification: Deduplication is a pure function operating on a list of linter findings (no I/O, no subprocess, no external dependencies). The logic can be tested in isolation by creating findings with identical (line, rule, issue) tuples and verifying the output contains only one instance. No integration with linting or GitHub is required.
behaviors.test_description: Create a list of linter findings where two different rules report the same issue on the same line (e.g., line 42: 'trailing whitespace' flagged by both rule-A and rule-B). Run deduplication logic. Verify output contains only one instance of line 42. Test non-dedup cases: different lines (no dedup), same line different issues (no dedup).
